ddls:
CREATE TABLE "datasets" (
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset */,
  "dataset_name" TEXT /* Name of the dataset */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dataset */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version number of the dataset */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the dataset was uploaded */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the dataset */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the dataset in MB */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who uploaded the dataset */,
  "tags" TEXT /* Tags associated with the dataset */,
  "categories" TEXT /* Categories the dataset belongs to */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the data */,
  "data_quality_issues" TEXT /* Data quality issues with the dataset */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "features" (
  "feature_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feature */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset the feature belongs to */,
  "feature_name" TEXT /* Name of the feature */,
  "feature_type" TEXT /* Type of the feature (e.g., numerical, categorical) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the feature */,
  "mean" REAL /* Mean value of the feature */,
  "std_dev" REAL /* Standard deviation of the feature */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum value of the feature */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum value of the feature */,
  "missing_value_count" INTEGER /* Number of missing values in the feature */,
  "distinct_value_count" INTEGER /* Number of distinct values in the feature */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feature_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_features_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data scientist, analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the user */,
  "team" TEXT /* Team the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the dataset */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the dataset was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "query_used" TEXT /* Query used to access the dataset */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the model */,
  "model_type" TEXT /* Type of the model (e.g., Linear Regression, Random Forest) */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset used to train the model */,
  "training_date" TEXT /* Date the model was trained */,
  "evaluation_metrics" TEXT /* Evaluation metrics of the model (e.g., RMSE, R^2) */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who trained the model */,
  "model_description" TEXT /* Description of the model */,
  "hyperparameters" TEXT /* Hyperparameters used to train the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_models_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_models_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_features" (
  "model_feature_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model-feature relationship */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model */,
  "feature_id" INTEGER /* ID of the feature */,
  "importance" REAL /* Importance of the feature in the model */,
  "feature_type" TEXT /* Type of the feature (e.g., numerical, categorical) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_feature_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_features_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_features_feature_id FOREIGN KEY ("feature_id") REFERENCES features ("feature_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_versions" (
  "model_version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model version */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version number of the model */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the model version */,
  "training_date" TEXT /* Date the model version was trained */,
  "evaluation_metrics" TEXT /* Evaluation metrics of the model version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_versions_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_performance" (
  "performance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each performance metric */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model */,
  "evaluation_date" TEXT /* Date when the performance was evaluated */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the performance metric (e.g., RMSE, R^2) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the performance metric */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset used for evaluation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("performance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_performance_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_performance_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prediction */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset used for prediction */,
  "prediction_date" TEXT /* Date when the prediction was made */,
  "predicted_value" REAL /* Predicted value */,
  "actual_value" REAL /* Actual value */,
  "confidence_interval" TEXT /* Confidence interval of the prediction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_predictions_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_predictions_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "dataset_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each dataset version */,
  "dataset_id" INTEGER /* ID of the dataset */,
  "version" TEXT /* Version number of the dataset */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the dataset version */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the dataset version was uploaded */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the dataset version */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the dataset version in MB */,
  "data_quality_issues" TEXT /* Data quality issues with the dataset version */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_dataset_versions_dataset_id FOREIGN KEY ("dataset_id") REFERENCES datasets ("dataset_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_explanations" (
  "explanation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each explanation */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model */,
  "feature_id" INTEGER /* ID of the feature */,
  "explanation_type" TEXT /* Type of explanation (e.g., SHAP, LIME) */,
  "explanation_value" TEXT /* Value of the explanation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("explanation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_explanations_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_explanations_feature_id FOREIGN KEY ("feature_id") REFERENCES features ("feature_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_collaborations" (
  "collaboration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each collaboration */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the collaboration (e.g., owner, contributor) */,
  "contribution" TEXT /* Contribution of the user to the model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("collaboration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_collaborations_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_collaborations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "model_comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment */,
  "model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the model */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "comment_date" TEXT /* Date when the comment was made */,
  "comment_text" TEXT /* Text of the comment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_model_comments_model_id FOREIGN KEY ("model_id") REFERENCES models ("model_id")
)
